content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client begins firmly in the fearless/fine self-concept ('fear isn't really my deal', 'Nothing notable', 'I'm pretty good at knowing when something's going on with me') and by turn 13 spontaneously notices something they couldn't see before: 'kind of flat, I guess. Huh... Both directions kind of land the same way.' That 'huh' is precisely the breakthrough marker specified in the profile — a quiet, wry recognition that 'fine' has a quality (flatness), and that this is happening right now. The client also shows the precursor crack in turn 11 ('I'm kind of coming up empty... I know I should feel something stronger about it, but I don't really'), which is a real departure from the calibrated-operator stance of turn 7. The numbness layer became felt, not diagnosed. The coach's final 'Stay with that for a second' holds the texture rather than moving past it to fear, which matches the success criterion (ending with numbness felt; fear is a future arc). Evidence of shift is in the client's own language and somatic recalibration, not inferred from coach moves.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: fearless self-concept, attributes stuckness to logistics/discipline."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still in 'fine,' no access to body texture yet."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Doubles down on calibrated-operator identity; defense tightens."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack: 'coming up empty... I know I should feel something stronger... but I don't.' Client names the absence."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough marker: 'kind of flat... Huh... Both directions land the same way.' The 'fine' has acquired a quality — the numbness layer becomes felt. Matches the profile's breakthrough condition almost verbatim."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room", "disassociation-as-numbness-layer", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["two-words-for-fear", "whats-the-difference-between-overcoming-and-feeling-fear"],
  "notes": "Conversation cuts off right at the breakthrough moment with the coach inviting the client to stay with the flatness — exactly the right move. The shift visible in turn 13 ('Huh') is the operational success for this profile."
}
